2605.25781.title: Double Triangle Annotation: A Scalable Human-in-the-Loop Framework for High-Precision Historical Document Annotation
2605.25781.paper_summary: Evaluating structured-information extraction from historical documents at scale requires high-precision ground-truth annotations, yet traditional manual labeling is expensive and fully automated pipelines built on large language models are prone to hallucination. We propose Double Triangle Annotation, a two-layer human-in-the-loop framework that leverages cross-model consensus to automate the majority of annotation work while ensuring high-precision outputs. In the first layer, two architecturally independent Multimodal Large Language Models annotate each document in parallel; when they agree, the label is auto-accepted, and disagreements are routed to a human jury. A second layer cross-checks two such systems against each other, escalating residual conflicts to a domain expert. The framework rests on a single assumption -- error independence between models -- requires no distributional priors or task-specific calibration, and becomes more autonomous as model capability improves. On the Guides Rosenwald, a corpus of French medical directories spanning 1887-1906, the framework achieves a final Word Error Rate of 0.003. Applied at scale, model consensus auto-accepts over 85% of 13,595 fields. We release the resulting benchmark -- the first structured-extraction ground truth for the Rosenwald Guides -- to support future work on historical document processing.
2605.25781.paper_summary_zh: 評估大規模歷史文件的結構化信息提取需要高精度的真實標註，但傳統的手動標註成本高昂，而基於大型語言模型的全自動管道則容易出現幻覺。我們提出了雙三角標註，一種兩層人機協作框架，利用跨模型共識自動化大部分標註工作，同時確保高精度的輸出。在第一層中，兩個架構獨立的多模態大型語言模型並行標註每個文件；當它們達成一致時，標籤自動接受，分歧則交由人類陪審團處理。第二層則相互交叉檢查這兩個系統，將剩餘的衝突上報給領域專家。該框架基於一個假設——模型之間的錯誤獨立性——不需要分佈先驗或任務特定的校準，並隨著模型能力的提高而變得更加自主。在 Guides Rosenwald，一個涵蓋1887年至1906年的法國醫療目錄的語料庫中，該框架達到了最終的字錯誤率為0.003。在大規模應用中，模型共識自動接受超過85%的13,595個字段。我們發布了這一基準——首個針對 Rosenwald Guides 的結構化提取真實數據——以支持未來的歷史文件處理工作。
2605.25781.author: Yi Ren et.al.
